states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
In ancient times, Bergaria was not a vast, imposing empire, but a scattered collection of tribes. The harsh mountains left little room to farm, so famines came both fierce and frequent. If they hoped to survive, peasants would have to swear their service to a warlord, turning their eyes away from the cruelty they inflicted.

The priestesses of Chronos, hidden in a northern temple, were spared their land's turmoil. In exchange for keeping watch over time's flow, the Primordial granted them both safety and power. In all of Erden, only they could manipulate time. 

Rolotia, gifted with the most prodigious time magic seen in generations, always felt that fate was more a curse than a blessing.

states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
As a child, Rolotia drove the other priestesses to no end of frustration. Though she was never malicious, never drunk on her own power, she proved too spirited to abide by the order's rules of isolation. Her eyes forever strayed to the horizon. Had she not been such a prodigy, perhaps the elders might have let her go. But doing so would have been too great a risk.

Before long, their indecision became their undoing. One morning, when the priestesses went to wake Rolotia for prayer, they found her staff gone and her bedroom empty. She had frozen time and escaped into the world so long forbidden to her.

states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
There was another rule that bound the priestesses of Chronos. No matter what tragedies befell mankind, they were never to reverse history once it had been decided. A single altered choice could change the future in ways no mortal could foresee.

After her escape, Rolotia cast aside her powers, aware of the temptation they would pose. But when her beloved—a general in the Bergarian army—fell, she rewrote time itself to bring him back.  The consequences of that sin would echo for a thousand years.

Even so, she greets each dawn with hope, for each new day offers her its chance of redemption.
